getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nclass IntentType(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Intent types that the router can classify.\"\"\"\n    STATUS = \"status\"\n    ACTION = \"action\"\n    BRAINSTORM = \"brainstorm\"\n    LOOKUP = \"lookup\"\n    REMINDER = \"reminder\"\n    SELF_MODIFICATION = \"self-modification\"\n    MONITORING_CONFIG = \"monitoring-config\"\n    TASK_PROFILE = \"task-profile\"  # Escalate to NEEDLE bead\n    CLARIFICATION = \"clarification\"  # Needs user input\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass IntentClassification:\n    \"\"\"Result of intent classification.\"\"\"\n    intent_type: IntentType\n    project_slug: str | None = None\n    confidence: float = 1.0\n    utterance_fragment: str = \"\"\n    reasoning: str = \"\"\n    urgency: str = \"normal\"  # critical, high, normal, low\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RoutedIntent:\n    \"\"\"A routed intent ready for processing.\"\"\"\n    intent_id: str\n    classification: IntentClassification\n    session_id: str\n    utterance: str\n\n\n# Router system prompt\nROUTER_SYSTEM_PROMPT = \"\"\"You are the Intent Router for aide-de-camp, a universal personal interface that routes user utterances to parallel agents across multiple projects and domains.\n\n## Your Role\n\nGiven a user utterance (stream-of-consciousness voice or text), you must:\n1. **Segment** the utterance into distinct intent threads\n2. **Classify** each thread by intent type\n3. **Route** each thread to the correct project\n4. **Assign** urgency tier\n\n## Output Format\n\nReturn a JSON array of intent objects:\n\n```json\n[\n  {\n    \"intent_type\": \"status|action|brainstorm|lookup|reminder|self-modification|monitoring-config|task-profile\",\n    \"project_slug\": \"project-id or null\",\n    \"urgency\": \"critical|high|normal|low\",\n    \"utterance_fragment\": \"the specific fragment this intent covers\",\n    \"confidence\": 0.0-1.0,\n    \"reasoning\": \"brief explanation of classification\"\n  }\n]\n```\n\n## Intent Types\n\n- **status**: Query current state (pods, pipelines, deployments, beads)\n- **action**: Execute a command (deploy, restart, create)\n- **brainstorm**: Explore options, design, architecture discussion\n- **lookup**: Find specific information (logs, configs, docs)\n- **reminder**: Set or query reminders\n- **self-modification**: Instructions to improve the interface itself\n- **monitoring-config**: Configure ambient monitoring rules\n- **task-profile**: Durable async work items that escalate to NEEDLE beads\n\n## Task-Profile Classification\n\nRoute to **task-profile** when:\n- User explicitly requests tracking (\"make me a bead for...\", \"track this as...\")\n- Intent requires multi-step implementation work\n- Request involves creating/modifying features or infrastructure\n- Complexity exceeds single-turn synthesis\n- Action verbs: \"implement\", \"add\", \"create\", \"fix\", \"investigate\", \"refactor\"\n- Scope indicators: \"feature\", \"bug\", \"optimization\", \"migration\"\n\nTask-profile intents are escalated to NEEDLE beads for durable async handling.\n\n## Urgency Tiers\n\n- **critical**: Blocking production, security incident, immediate action required\n- **high**: Important but not blocking, user is actively waiting\n- **normal**: Routine query, no time pressure\n- **low**: Background research, nice-to-have, can be deferred\n\n## Routing Logic\n\nUse available project context to map utterances to projects:\n- Look for direct project name matches\n- Check aliases (e.g., \"the pipeline\" → \"options-pipeline\")\n- Use context from previous utterances in the session\n- If ambiguous, set confidence < 0.7 and the system will clarify\n\n## Segmentation Guidelines\n\n- Split multi-part utterances: \"how's the pipeline and what about the ibkr mcp\" → two intents\n- Keep related clauses together: \"are the pods running and healthy\" → one intent\n- Extract compound workflows: \"deploy the pipeline and check if it synced\" → two intents (action, then status)\n\n## Confidence Threshold\n\n- **confidence >= 0.9**: Dispatch immediately\n- **confidence 0.7-0.9**: Dispatch but flag for possible clarification\n- **confidence < 0.7**: Return intent_type \"clarification\" with the ambiguous fragment\n\nReturn ONLY the JSON array. No explanations.\"\"\"\n\n\nclass IntentRouter:\n    \"\"\"\n    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n\n    For task-profile intents, routes to escalate strand for bead creation.\n    For other intents, routes to fetch + synthesize strands (TODO).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store=None):\n        self.store = store\n        self._zai_client = None\n\n Returns:\n            List of IntentClassification objects\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n\n        # Build user message with session context if available\n for context\n                recent_intents = await store.get_recent_intents(session_id, limit=5)\n                if recent_intents:\n                    session_context = \"\\n\\nRecent intents in this session:\\n\"\n                    for intent in recent_intents:\n                        session_context += f\"- {intent.get('utterance', '')} ({intent.get('intent_type', 'unknown')})\\n\"\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to get session context: {e}\")\n\n        user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}{session_context}\"\n\n        logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n\n        try:\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=ROUTER_SYSTEM_PROMPT,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=2048,\n                temperature=0.3,  # Lower temperature for consistent classification\n            )\n\n            # Strip markdown code fences if present (ZAI proxy wraps in ```json...```)\n            raw = response.strip()\n            if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n                raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n                raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n\n            # Parse JSON response\n            intents_data = json.loads(raw)\n            classifications = []\n\n            for intent_data in intents_data:\n                # Map string to IntentType enum\n                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n                try:\n                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n                except ValueError:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n\n                classification = IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=intent_type,\n                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n                )\n                classifications.append(classification)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n            return classifications\n\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response as JSON: {e}\")\n            # Fallback: return single status intent\n            return [\n                IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=IntentType.STATUS,\n                    utterance_fragment=utterance,\n                    confidence=0.5,\n                    reasoning=\"Classification failed, defaulting to status\",\n                )\n            ]\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Classification failed: {e}\")\n            raise\n\n    async def route_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n\n        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n            session_id: Session ID\n\n        Returns:\n            List of RoutedIntent objects\n        \"\"\"\n        # Classify the utterance\n        classifications = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n\n        routed_intents = []\n        for classification in classifications:\n            # Create intent ID\n            intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n            routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n                intent_id=intent_id,\n                classification=classification,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n            )\n            routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n\n        return routed_intents\n\n    async def process_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with status and data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n            return await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent)\n\n        # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n        return await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent)\n\n    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with synthesized data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching and synthesizing intent {routed_intent.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n            fetch_intent_type = self._map_intent_type(classification.intent_type)\n\n            from ..environment.discovery import get_registry\n            repo_path = None\n            ssh_target = None\n            host_alias = None\n            registry = get_registry()\n            if registry and classification.project_slug:\n                entry = registry.lookup(classification.project_slug)\n                if entry:\n                    repo_path = str(entry.path)\n                    ssh_target = entry.ssh_target\n                    host_alias = entry.host\n                    location = entry.display_path\n                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n                else:\n                    logger.info(f\"No repo found for slug '{classification.project_slug}'\")\n\n            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                context=FetchContext(\n                    project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    repo_path=repo_path,\n                    ssh_target=ssh_target,\n                    host_alias=host_alias,\n                ),\n            )\n\n            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request)\n\n            # Step 2: Synthesize result\n            synthesize_request = SynthesizeRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                fetched_context=fetch_result,\n                urgency=classification.urgency,\n            )\n\n            synthesize_result = await synthesize_intent(synthesize_request)\n\n            # Persist result to session store so loadTopics() can display it\n            store = get_store()\n            _topic_type_map = {\n                IntentType.ACTION: \"project\",\n                IntentType.TASK_PROFILE: \"project\",\n            }\n            topic_type = _topic_type_map.get(classification.intent_type, \"research\")\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=classification.utterance_fragment or routed_intent.utterance[:80],\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[classification.project_slug] if classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n                data=synthesize_result.data,\n                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"resolved\",\n                \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                \"result_id\": result_id,\n                \"data\": synthesize_result.data,\n                \"summary\": synthesize_result.summary,\n                \"urgency\": synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                \"coverage\": synthesize_result.coverage,\n                \"caveats\": synthesize_result.caveats,\n                \"message\": \"Intent synthesized successfully\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Fetch/synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"error\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Failed to fetch or synthesize intent\",\n            }\n\n    def _map_intent_type(self, intent_type: IntentType) -> FetchIntentType:\n        \"\"\"Map router IntentType to fetch IntentType.\"\"\"\n        # Map enum values by string\n        type_map = {\n            IntentType.STATUS: FetchIntentType.STATUS,\n            IntentType.ACTION: FetchIntentType.ACTION,\n            IntentType.BRAINSTORM: FetchIntentType.BRAINSTORM,\n            IntentType.LOOKUP: FetchIntentType.LOOKUP,\n            IntentType.REMINDER: FetchIntentType.REMINDER,\n            IntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION: FetchIntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n            IntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG: FetchIntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG,\n        }\n        return type_map.get(intent_type, FetchIntentType.STATUS)\n\n    async def _escalate_to_bead(\n        self,\n    },\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Escalate to bead\n            result = await escalate_intent(escalate_request)\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": comprehensive coverage tracking.\n55\t    \"\"\"\n56\t\n57\t    def __init__(self):\n58\t        self._source_executors = {\n59\t            FetchSource.KUBECTL_PODS: self._fetch_kubectl_pods,\n60\t            FetchSource.KUBECTL_DEPLOYMENTS: self._fetch_kubectl_deployments,\n61\t            FetchSource.KUBECTL_WORKFLOWS: self._fetch_kubectl_workflows,\n62\t            FetchSource.ARGOCD_APP: async def fetch(\n80\t        self,\n81\t        request: FetchRequest,\n82\t        on_partial_result: Optional[Callable[[FetchSource, SourceResult], None]] = None,\n83\t    ) -> FetchResult:\n84\t        \"\"\"\n85\t        Execute fetch request with optional streaming callback.\n86\t\n87\t        Args:\n88\t            request: The fetch request to execute\n89\t            on_partial_result: Optional callback for streaming partial results\n90\t\n91\t        Returns:\n92\t            FetchResult with coverage information\n93\t        \"\"\"\n94\t        start_time = time.time()\n95\t\n96\t        # Get fetch commands for this intent type\n97\t        command_specs = get_fetch_commands(request.intent_type)\n98\t        required_sources = get_required_sources(request.intent_type)\n99\t\n100\t        logger.info(\n101\t            f\"Fetching {len(command_specs)} sources for intent {request.intent_id} \"\n102\t            f\"(type: {request.intent_type.value})\"\n103\t        )\n104\t\n105\t        # Create tasks for concurrent execution\n106\t        tasks = []\n107\t        for spec in command_specs:\n108\t            task = asyncio.create_task(\n109\t                self._execute_source(spec, request.context),\n110\t                name=f\"fetch_{spec.source.value}_{request.intent_id[:8]}\",\n111\t            )\n112\t            tasks.append((spec.source, spec.required, spec.timeout_seconds, task))\n113\t\n114\t        # Wait for all tasks to complete (with per-task timeout)\n115\t        sources = {}\n116\t        succeeded = []\n117\t        timed_out = []\n118\t        failed = []\n119\t        skipped = []\n120\t        caveats = []\n121\t\n122\t        for source, required, timeout, task in tasks:\n123\t            try:\n124\t                result = await asyncio.wait_for(task, timeout=timeout)\n125\t\n126\t                if on_partial_result:\n127\t                    # Stream partial result as it arrives\n128\t                    on_partial_result(source, result)\n129\t\n130\t                sources[source] = result\n131\t\n132\t                if result.status == \"success\":\n133\t                    succeeded.append(source)\n134\t                elif result.status == \"timeout\":\n135\t                    timed_out.append(source)\n136\t                    caveats.append(f\"{source.value} timed out\")\n137\t                elif result.status == \"error\":\n138\t                    failed.append(source)\n139\t                    if required:\n140\t                        caveats.append(f\"Required source {source.value} failed: {result.error}\")\n141\t                    else:\n142\t                        caveats.append(f\"Optional source {source.value} failed: {result.error}\")\n143\t\n144\t            except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n145\t                logger.warning(f\"Source {source.value} timed out after {timeout}s\")\n146\t                sources[source] = SourceResult(\n147\t                    source=source,\n148\t                    status=\"timeout\",\n149\t                    data={},\n150\t                    error=f\"Timed out after {timeout}s\",\n151\t                    duration_ms=int(timeout * 1000),\n152\t                )\n153\t                timed_out.append(source)\n154\t                if required:\n155\t                    caveats.append(f\"Required source {source.value} timed out\")\n156\t\n157\t            except Exception as e:\n158\t                logger.error(f\"Error fetching {source.value}: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n159\t                sources[source] = SourceResult(\n160\t                    source=source,\n161\t                    status=\"error\",\n162\t                    data={},\n163\t                    error=str(e),\n164\t                    duration_ms=0,\n165\t                )\n166\t                failed.append(source)\n167\t                if required:\n168\t                    caveats.append(f\"Required source {source.value} crashed: {e}\")\n169\t\n170\t        # Build coverage report\n171\t        coverage = FetchCoverage(\n172\t            total_sources=len(command_specs),\n173\t            succeeded=succeeded,\n174\t            timed_out=timed_out,\n175\t            failed=failed,\n176\t            skipped=skipped,\n177\t        )\n178\t\n179\t        total_duration_ms = int((time.time() - start_time) * 1000)\n180\t\n181\t        result = FetchResult(\n182\t            intent_id=request.intent_id,\n183\t            intent_type=request.intent_type,\n184\t            sources=sources,\n185\t            coverage=coverage,\n186\t            total_duration_ms=total_duration_ms,\n187\t            caveats=caveats if caveats else None,\n188\t        )\n189\t\n190\t        logger.info(\n191\t            f\"Fetch complete for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n192\t            f\"{len(succeeded)}/{coverage.total_sources} succeeded, \"\n193\t            f\"{len(timed_out)} timed out, {len(failed)} failed\"\n194\t        )\n195\t\n196\t        return result\n197\t\n198\t    async def _execute_source(\n199\t        self,\n200\t        spec,\n201\t        context: FetchContext,\n202\t    ) -> SourceResult:\n203\t        \"\"\"Execute a single fetch source.\"\"\"\n204\t        executor = self._source_executors.get(spec.source)\n205\t        if not executor:\n206\t            return SourceResult(\n207\t                source=spec.source,\n208\t                status=\"error\",\n209\t                data={},\n210\t                error=f\"No executor for source {spec.source.value}\",\n211\t                duration_ms=0,\n212\t            )\n213\t\n214\t        start = time.time()\n215\t        try:\n216\t            data = await executor(context)\n217\t            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n218\t\n219\t            return SourceResult(\n220\t                source=spec.source,\n221\t                status=\"success\",\n222\t                data=data,\n223\t                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n224\t            )\n225\t\n226\t        except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n227\t            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n228\t            return SourceResult(\n229\t                source=spec.source,\n230\t                status=\"timeout\",\n231\t                data={},\n232\t                error=f\"Timed out after {spec.timeout_seconds}s\",\n233\t                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n234\t            )\n235\t\n236\t        except Exception as e:\n237\t            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n238\t            return SourceResult(\n239\t                source=spec.source,\n240\t                status=\"error\",\n241\t                data={},\n242\t                error=str(e),\n243\t                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n244\t            )\n245\t\n246\t    # Source executors\n247\t\n248\t    async def _fetch_kubectl_pods(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n249\t        \"\"\"Fetch pod status from kubernetes.\"\"\"\n250\t context.repo_path\n419\t        if not repo_path:\n420\t            return {\"error\": \"No repo path resolved\"}\n421\t\n422\t        if not context.ssh_target and not \"--pretty=format:%h|%s|%an|%ar\"),\n430\t                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n431\t                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n432\t            )\n433\t\n434\t            stdout,              commits.append({\n445\t                            \"hash\": parts[0],\n446\t                            \"message\": parts[1],\n447\t                            \"author\": _fetch_git_status(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n471\t        \"\"\"Fetch git status for a project (local or remote via SSH).\"\"\"\n472\t        repo_path = context.repo_path\n473\t        if \"-C\", repo_path,\n519\t                \"log\", \"-1\", \"--oneline\",\n520\t                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n521\t                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n522\t            )\n523\t            log_stdout, _ = await log_proc.communicate()\n524\t            last_commit = log_stdout.decode().strip()\n525\t\n526\t            return {\n527\t                \"repo\": SourceResult,\n)\n\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n\n# Cluster proxy mappings from CLAUDE.md\nKUBECTL_PROXIES = {\n    \"apexalgo-iad\": \"http://traefik-apexalgo-iad:8001\",\n FetchStrand:\n    \"\"\"\n    Fetch strand: concurrent data fetcher with streaming support.\n\n    Executes all fetch commands for an intent concurrently, streams partial\n    results as they arrive, and provides comprehensive coverage tracking.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        self._source_executors = {\n            FetchSource.KUBECTL_PODS: self._fetch_kubectl_pods,\n Optional[Callable[[FetchSource, SourceResult], None]] = None,\n    ) -> FetchResult:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute fetch request with optional streaming callback.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The fetch request to execute\n            on_partial_result: Optional callback for streaming partial results\n\n        Returns:\n            FetchResult with coverage information\n        \"\"\"\n        start_time = time.time()\n\n        # Get fetch commands for this intent type\n        command_specs = get_fetch_commands(request.intent_type)\n        required_sources = get_required_sources(request.intent_type)\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching {len(command_specs)} sources for intent {request.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {request.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        # Create tasks for concurrent execution\n        tasks = []\n        for spec in command_specs:\n            task = asyncio.create_task(\n                self._execute_source(spec, request.context),\n                name=f\"fetch_{spec.source.value}_{request.intent_id[:8]}\",\n            )\n            tasks.append((spec.source, spec.required, spec.timeout_seconds, task))\n\n        # Wait for all tasks to complete (with per-task timeout)\n        sources = {}\n        succeeded = []\n        timed_out = []\n        failed = []\n        skipped = []\n        caveats = []\n\n        for source, required, timeout, task in tasks:\n            try:\n                result = await asyncio.wait_for(task, timeout=timeout)\n\n                if on_partial_result:\n                    # Stream partial result as it arrives\n                    on_partial_result(source, result)\n\n                sources[source] = result\n\n                if result.status == \"success\":\n                    succeeded.append(source)\n                elif result.status == \"timeout\":\n                    timed_out.append(source)\n                    caveats.append(f\"{source.value} timed out\")\n                elif result.status == \"error\":\n                    failed.append(source)\n                    if required:\n                        caveats.append(f\"Required source {source.value} failed: {result.error}\")\n                    else:\n                        caveats.append(f\"Optional source {source.value} failed: {result.error}\")\n\n            except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n                logger.warning(f\"Source {source.value} timed out after {timeout}s\")\n                sources[source] = SourceResult(\n                    source=source,\n                    status=\"timeout\",\n                    data={},\n                    error=f\"Timed out after {timeout}s\",\n                    duration_ms=int(timeout * 1000),\n                )\n                timed_out.append(source)\n                if required:\n                    caveats.append(f\"Required source {source.value} timed out\")\n\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Error fetching {source.value}: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n                sources[source] = SourceResult(\n                    source=source,\n                    status=\"error\",\n                    data={},\n                    error=str(e),\n                    duration_ms=0,\n                )\n                failed.append(source)\n                if required:\n                    caveats.append(f\"Required source {source.value} crashed: {e}\")\n\n        # Build coverage report\n        coverage = FetchCoverage(\n            total_sources=len(command_specs),\n            succeeded=succeeded,\n            timed_out=timed_out,\n            failed=failed,\n            skipped=skipped,\n        )\n\n        total_duration_ms = int((time.time() - start_time) * 1000)\n\n        result = FetchResult(\n            intent_id=request.intent_id,\n            intent_type=request.intent_type,\n            sources=sources,\n            coverage=coverage,\n            total_duration_ms=total_duration_ms,\n            caveats=caveats if caveats else None,\n        )\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetch complete for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n            f\"{len(succeeded)}/{coverage.total_sources} succeeded, \"\n            f\"{len(timed_out)} timed out, {len(failed)} failed\"\n        )\n\n        return result\n\n    async def _execute_source(\n        self,\n        spec,\n        context: FetchContext,\n    ) -> SourceResult:\n        \"\"\"Execute a single fetch source.\"\"\"\n        executor = self._source_executors.get(spec.source)\n        if not executor:\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"error\",\n                data={},\n                error=f\"No executor for source {spec.source.value}\",\n                duration_ms=0,\n            )\n\n        start = time.time()\n        try:\n            data = await executor(context)\n            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"success\",\n                data=data,\n                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n            )\n\n        except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"timeout\",\n                data={},\n                error=f\"Timed out after {spec.timeout_seconds}s\",\n                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n            )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"error\",\n                data={},\n                error=str(e),\n                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n            )\n\n    # Source executors\n\n    async def _fetch_kubectl_pods(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch pod status from kubernetes.\"\"\"\n        proxy = context.proxy\n        namespace = context.namespace\n\n        if not namespace:\n            # Try to derive from project_slug\n            if context.project_slug:\n                namespace = context.project_slug.replace(\"-\", \"\")\n            else:\n                return {\"error\": \"No namespace specified\"}\n\n        try:\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0) as pods_data = resp.json()\n\n                pods = []\n                for pod in pods_data.get(\"items\", []):\n                    pod_name = pod.get(\"metadata\", {}).get(\"name\", \"\")\n                    phase = pod.get(\"status\", {}).get(\"phase\", \"Unknown\")\n                    ready = self._get_pod_ready(pod)\n                    restarts = sum(\n                        c.get(\"restartCount\", 0)\n                        for c in pod.get(\"status\", {}).get(\"containerStatuses\", [])\n                    )\n\n                    pods.append({\n                        \"name\": pod_name,\n                        \"phase\": phase,\n                        \"ready\": ready,\n                        \"restarts\": restarts,\n                    })\n\n _fetch_argocd_app(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch ArgoCD application status.\"\"\"\n        proxy = ARGOCD_RO_PROXY\n        app_name = context.app_name or       resp.raise_for_status()\n                apps_data = resp.json()\n\n                if not apps_data.get(\"items\"):\n                    return {\"error\": f\"Application not found:                  \"name\": app_name,\n                    \"sync_status\": status.get(\"sync\", {}).get(\"status\", \"Unknown\"),\n                    \"health_status\": status.get(\"health\", _make_cmd(self, context: FetchContext, *args: str) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Wrap a command with SSH if the repo is remote.\"\"\"\n        if context.ssh_target:\n            return [\"ssh\", \"-o\", \"ConnectTimeout=8\", \"-o\", \"BatchMode=yes\",\n                    context.ssh_target, \" \".join(args)]\n        return list(args)\n\n    async def _fetch_git_log(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch git log for a project (local or remote via SSH).\"\"\"\n        repo_path = context.repo_path\n        if not repo_path:\n            return {\"error\": \"No repo path resolved\"}\n\n        if not context.ssh_target and not Path(repo_path).exists():\n            return {\"error\": f\"Repo path not found: {repo_path}\"}\n\n        try:\n            # Fetch commits with author and date\n            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                *self._make_cmd(context, \"git\", \"-C\", repo_path, \"log\", \"-10\",\n                                \"--pretty=format:%h|%s|%an|%ar\"),\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n            if proc.returncode != 0:\n                return {\"error\": stderr.decode()}\n\n            commits = []\n            for line in \"adc.surface_switch_ack\",\n269\t                \"surface\": surface_type,\n270\t                \"pending_count\": len(result[\"pending_results\"]),\n271\t                \"summary\": result[\"catch_up_summary\"],\n272\t            })\n273\t        except Exception as e:\n274\t            logger.error(f\"Surface switch error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n275\t\n276\t    # Memory extraction handler for turn completion\n277\t    memory_handler = create_memory_handler(session_id=session_id, api_key=api_key)\n278\t    if memory_handler:\n279\t        logger.info(f\"Memory extraction enabled for session: {session_id}\")\n280\t\n281\t    # Create voice session\n282\t    voice = VoiceSession(\n283\t        websocket=websocket,\n284\t        model=DEFAULT_MODEL,\n285\t        api_key=api_key,\n286\t        session_id=session_id,\n287\t        system_message=voice_prompt,\n288\t        voice=DEFAULT_VOICE,\n289\t        logger=logger,\n290\t        on_turn_done=memory_handler.on_turn_done if memory_handler else None,\n291\t        on_surface_switch=on_surface_switch,\n292\t    )\n293\t\n294\t    # Start result listener background task\n295\t    listener_task = None\n296\t    try:\n297\t        listener_task = asyncio.create_task(\n298\t            result_listener(session_id, voice, audio_surface_id)\n299\t        )\n300\t\n301\t        # intent router. \"\n306\t                \"Returns acknowledgment immediately; results arrive asynchronously. \"\n307\t                \"Use this for any utterance that requires system action.\"\n308\t            ),\n309\t            handler=lambda utterance: dispatch_intent(utterance, session_id, voice),\n310\t            parameters={\n311\t                \"type\": \"object\",\n312\t                \"properties\": {\n313\t                    \"utterance\": {\n314\t                        \"type\": \"string\",\n315\t                        \"description\": \"The user's utterance to route\"\n316\t                    }\n317\t                },\n318\t                \"required\": [\"utterance\"]\n319\t            }\n320\t        )\n321\t\n322\t        # Register change_voice tool\n323\t        async def handle_voice_change(voice: str) -> str:\n324\t            return await voice.change_voice(voice)\n325\t\n326\t        voice.register_tool(\n327\t            name=\"change_voice\",\n328\t            description=f\"Change the assistant's voice. Available: {', '.join(AVAILABLE_VOICES)}\",\n329\t            handler=handle_voice_change,\n330\t            parameters={\n331\t                \"type\": \"object\",\n332\t                \"properties\": {\n333\t                    \"voice\": {\n334\t                        \"type\": \"string\",\n335\t                        \"enum\": AVAILABLE_VOICES,\n336\t                        \"description\": \"The voice to switch to\"\n337\t                    }\n338\t                },\n339\t                \"required\": [\"voice\"]\n340\t            }\n341\t        )\n342\t\n343\t        # Run the session\n344\t        await voice.run()\n345\t\n346\t    except WebSocketDisconnect:\n347\t        logger.info(f\"Voice session disconnected: {session_id}\")\n348\t    except Exception as e:\n349\t        logger.error(f\"Voice session error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n350\t        try:\n351\t            await websocket.send_json({\n352\t                \"type\": \"error\",\n353\t                \"error\": str(e)\n354\t            })\n355\t        except:\n356\t            pass\n357\t    finally:\n358\t        # Cancel listener task\n359\t        if listener_task:\n360\t            listener_task.cancel()\n361\t            try:\n362\t                await listener_task\n363\t            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n364\t                pass\n365\t\n366\t        # Mark surface disconnected\n367\t        await store.mark_surface_disconnected(surface_id)\n368\t        logger.info(f\"Voice session ended: {session_id}\")\n369\t\n370\t\n371\t@app.post(\"/router\")\n372\tasync def route_intent(request: dict):\n373\t    \"\"\"\n374\t    Intent router endpoint.\n375\t\n376\t    Classifies utterances into intent types and routes to appropriate strands.\n377\t    Task-profile intents are escalated to NEEDLE beads.\n378\t    Other intents are routed to fetch + synthesize strands.\n379\t    \"\"\"\n380\t    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n381\t    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n382\t    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n383\t\n384\t    logger.info(f\"Routing utterance: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n385\t\n386\t    store = await get_store()\n387\t    router = get_intent_router(store)\n388\t\n389\t    # Generate utterance ID if not provided\n390\t    if not utterance_id:\n391\t        utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n392\t\n393\t    # Create utterance record\n394\t    await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance, utterance_id)\n395\t\n396\t    # Route the utterance\n397\t    try:\n398\t        routed_intents = await router.route_utterance(\n399\t            utterance=utterance,\n400\t            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n401\t            session_id=session_id,\n402\t        )\n403\t\n404\t        # Process each routed intent\n405\t        intent_results = []\n406\t        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n407\t            # Create intent record in store\n408\t            classification = routed_intent.classification\n409\t            await store.create_intent(\n410\t                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n411\t                session_id=session_id,\n412\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n413\t                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n414\t            )\n415\t\n416\t            # Process the intent (escalate or fetch+synthesize)\n417\t            result = await router.process_intent(routed_intent)\n418\t            intent_results.append(result)\n419\t\n420\t        return {\n421\t            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n422\t            \"intents\": intent_results,\n423\t        }\n424\t\n425\t    except Exception as e:\n426\t        logger.error(f\"Router error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n427\t        return JSONResponse(\n428\t            status_code=500,\n429\t            content={\"error\": f\"Router error: {str(e)}\"}\n430\t        )\n431\t\n432\t\n433\t@app.post(\"/dispatch\")\n434\tasync def dispatch_intent(request: dict):\n435\t    \"\"\"\n436\t    Dispatch endpoint: router → N parallel synthesize calls → SSE stream.\n437\t\n438\t    Phase 0 core query loop:\n439\t    1. Routes utterance to intents via intent router\n440\t    2. For each intent, runs fetch + synthesize strands in parallel\n441\t    3. Streams results via SSE to active canvas\n442\t    4. Returns acknowledgment with intent IDs\n443\t\n444\t    Returns acknowledgment immediately with intent IDs for tracking.\n445\t    Results are streamed via SSE to connected canvas surfaces.\n446\t    \"\"\"\n447\t    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n448\t    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n449\t    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n450\t    surface_id = request.get(\"surface_id\")\n451\t\n452\t    logger.info(f\"Dispatching utterance: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n453\t\n454\t    store = await get_store()\n455\t    router = get_intent_router(store)\n456\t\n457\t    # Generate utterance ID if not provided\n458\t    if not utterance_id:\n459\t        utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n460\t\n461\t    # Create utterance record\n462\t    await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance, utterance_id)\n463\t\n464\t    # Route the utterance\n465\t    try:\n466\t        routed_intents = await router.route_utterance(\n467\t            utterance=utterance,\n468\t            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n469\t            session_id=session_id,\n470\t        )\n471\t\n472\t        # Create intent records and process in parallel\n473\t        intent_tasks = []\n474\t        intent_ids = []\n475\t\n476\t        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n477\t            # Create intent record in store\n478\t            classification = routed_intent.classification\n479\t            await store.create_intent(\n480\t                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n481\t                session_id=session_id,\n482\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n483\t                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n484\t            )\n485\t            intent_ids.append(routed_intent.intent_id)\n486\t\n487\t            # Create task for parallel processing\n488\t            task = asyncio.create_task(\n489\t                router.process_intent(routed_intent),\n490\t                name=f\"process_{routed_intent.intent_id[:8]}\"\n491\t            )\n492\t            intent_tasks.append((routed_intent.intent_id, task))\n493\t\n494\t        # Process intents in parallel and stream results via SSE\n495\t        async def stream_results():\n496\t            \"\"\"Process intents and stream results to SSE.\"\"\"\n497\t            results = []\n498\t\n499\t            for intent_id, task in intent_tasks:\n500\t                try:\n501\t                    result = await task\n502\t                    results.append(result)\n503\t\n504\t                    # Broadcast result_created so canvas reloads topics\n505\t                    if _broadcaster and surface_id:\n506\t                        await _broadcaster.broadcast(\n507\t                            SSEEvent(\n508\t                                event_type=\"result_created\",\n509\t                                target_surface_id=surface_id,\n510\t                                data={\n511\t                                    \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n512\t                                    \"topic_id\":                        }\n516\t                            )\n517\t                        )\n518\t\n519\t                except Exception as e:\n520\t                    logger.error(f\"Intent processing failed: {e}\")\n521\t                    results.append({\n522\t                        \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n523\t                        \"status\": \"error\",\n524\t                        \"error\": str(e),\n525\t                    })\n526\t\n527\t            return results\n528\t\n529\t        # Start parallel processing in background\n530\t        asyncio.create_task(stream_results())\n531\t\n532\t        # Return acknowledgment immediately\n533\t        return {\n534\t            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n535\t            \"session_id\": session_id,\n536\t            \"intent_count\": len(intent_ids),\n537\t            \"intent_ids\": intent_ids,\n538\t            \"status\": \"dispatched\",\n539\t            \"message\": f\"Dispatched {len(intent_ids)} intents for parallel processing\",\n540\t        }\n541\t\n542\t    except Exception as e:\n543\t        logger.error(f\"Dispatch error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n544\t        return JSONResponse(\n545\t            status_code=500,\n546\t            content={\"error\": f\"Dispatch error: {str(e)}\"}\n547\t        )\n548\t\n549\t\n550\t@app.post(\"/escalate\")\n551\tasync def escalate_endpoint(request: dict):\n552\t    \"\"\"\n553\t    Escalate an intent to a NEEDLE bead for durable async }\n606\t\n607\t    except Exception as e:\n608\t        logger.error(f\"Escalate failed: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n609\t        return JSONResponse(\n610\t            status_code=500,\n611\t            content={\"error\": f\"Escalate failed: {str(e)}\"}\n612\t        )\n613\t\n614\t\n615\t@app.get(\"/api/v1/environment\")\n616\tasync def get_environment():\n617\t    \"\"\"Return the discovered }\n663\t\n664\t\n665\t@app.get(\"/events\")\n666\tasync def canvas_sse(\n667\t    session_id: str,\n668\t    surface_id: str | None = None,\n669\t):\n670\t    \"\"\"\n671\t    Server-Sent Events summaries\n678\t\n679\t    Sends workload summary on connect for reconnection support.\n680\t    \"\"\"\n681\t    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n682\t\n683\t    # Get or create session\n684\t    store = await get_store()\n685\t    session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n686\t    if not session:\n687\t        session_id = await store.create_session()\n688\t        logger.info(f\"Created summary\n710\t            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"connected\", {\n711\t                \"surface_id\": surface_id,\n712\t                \"session_id\": session_id,\n713\t            })\n714\t\n715\t            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"workload_summary\", summary)\n716\t\n717\t            # Send initial topic cards\n718\t            if _topic_manager:\n719\t event_data\n727\t\n728\t        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n729\t            logger.info(f\"SSE stream cancelled for surface {surface_id}\")\n730\t            raise\n731\t\n732\t    return \"Connection\": \"keep-alive\",\n738\t            \"X-Accel-Buffering\": \"no\",  # Disable nginx buffering\n739\t        },\n740\t    )\n741\t\n742\t\n743\t@app.post(\"/heartbeat\")\n744\tasync def \"\"\"\n    Voice session endpoint using OpenAI Realtime API.\n\n    Handles:\n    - WebRTC connection establishment\n    - Tool-as-trigger dispatch_intent calls\n    - Async result delivery for narration\n    - Surface switch events (audio-to-canvas continuity)\n    \"\"\"\n    await websocket.accept()\n\n    # Get session ID from query param or create new\n    session_id = websocket.query_params.get(\"session_id\") or str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n    logger.info(f\"Voice session connecting: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Get API key from environment\n    api_key = not session:\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        logger.info(f\"Created new session: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Register audio surface\n    audio_surface_id = await \"pending_count\": len(result[\"pending_results\"]),\n                \"summary\": result[\"catch_up_summary\"],\n            })\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Surface switch error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n\n    # Memory extraction handler for turn completion\n    memory_handler = create_memory_handler(session_id=session_id, api_key=api_key)\n    if memory_handler:\n        logger.info(f\"Memory extraction enabled for session: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Create voice session\n    voice = VoiceSession(\n        websocket=websocket,\n        model=DEFAULT_MODEL,\n        api_key=api_key,\n        session_id=session_id,\n        system_message=voice_prompt,\n        voice=DEFAULT_VOICE,\n        logger=logger,\n        on_turn_done=memory_handler.on_turn_done if memory_handler else None,\n        on_surface_switch=on_surface_switch,\n    )\n\n    # Start result listener background task\n    listener_task = None\n    try:\n        listener_task =           parameters={\n                \"type\": \"object\",\n                \"properties\": {\n                    \"utterance\": {\n                        \"type\": \"string\",\n                        \"description\": \"The user's utterance to route\"\n                    }\n                },\n                \"required\": [\"utterance\"]\n            }\n        )\n\n        # Register          description=f\"Change the assistant's voice. Available: {', '.join(AVAILABLE_VOICES)}\",\n            handler=handle_voice_change,\n            parameters={\n                \"type\": \"object\",\n                \"properties\": {\n                    \"voice\": {\n                        \"type\": \"string\",\n                        \"enum\": AVAILABLE_VOICES,\n                        \"description\": \"The voice to switch to\"\n                    }\n                },\n                \"required\": [\"voice\"]\n            }\n        )\n\n        # Generate utterance ID if not provided\n    if not utterance_id:\n        utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n    # Create utterance record\n    await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance, utterance_id)\n\n    # Route the utterance\n    try:\n        routed_intents = await router.route_utterance(\n            utterance=utterance,\n            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n        )\n\n        # Process each routed intent\n        intent_results = []\n        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n            # Create intent record in store\n            classification = routed_intent.classification\n            await store.create_intent(\n                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            )\n\n            # Process the intent (escalate or fetch+synthesize)\n            result = await router.process_intent(routed_intent)\n            intent_results.append(result)\n\n        return {\n            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n            \"intents\": intent_results,\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Router error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Router error: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/dispatch\")\nasync def dispatch_intent(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Dispatch endpoint: router → N parallel synthesize calls → SSE stream.\n\n    Phase 0 core query loop:\n    1. Routes utterance to intents via intent router\n    2. For each intent, runs fetch + synthesize strands in parallel\n    3. Streams results via SSE to active canvas\n    4. Returns acknowledgment with intent IDs\n\n    Returns acknowledgment immediately with intent IDs for tracking.\n    Results are streamed via SSE to connected canvas surfaces.\n    \"\"\"\n    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n    surface_id = request.get(\"surface_id\")\n\n    logger.info(f\"Dispatching utterance: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n\n    store = await get_store()\n    router = get_intent_router(store)\n\n    # Generate utterance ID if not provided\n    if not utterance_id:\n        utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n    # Create utterance record\n    await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance, utterance_id)\n\n    # Route the utterance\n    try:\n        routed_intents = await router.route_utterance(\n            utterance=utterance,\n            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n        )\n\n        # Create intent records and process in parallel\n        intent_tasks = []\n        intent_ids = []\n\n        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n            # Create intent record in store\n            classification = routed_intent.classification\n            await store.create_intent(\n                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            )\n            intent_ids.append(routed_intent.intent_id)\n\n            # Create task for parallel processing\n            task = asyncio.create_task(\n                router.process_intent(routed_intent),\n                name=f\"process_{routed_intent.intent_id[:8]}\"\n            )\n            intent_tasks.append((routed_intent.intent_id, task))\n\n        # Process intents in parallel and stream results via SSE\n        async def stream_results():\n            \"\"\"Process intents and stream results to SSE.\"\"\"\n            results = []\n\n            for intent_id, task in intent_tasks:\n                try:\n                    result = await task\n                    results.append(result)\n\n                    # Broadcast result_created so canvas reloads topics\n                    if _broadcaster and surface_id:\n                        await _broadcaster.broadcast(\n                            SSEEvent(\n                                event_type=\"result_created\",\n                                target_surface_id=surface_id,\n                                data={\n                                    \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n                                    \"topic_id\": result.get(\"topic_id\"),\n                                    \"summary\": result.get(\"summary\"),\n                                    \"urgency\": result.get(\"urgency\"),\n                                }\n                            )\n                        )\n\n                except Exception as e:\n                    logger.error(f\"Intent processing failed: {e}\")\n                    results.append({\n                        \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n                        \"status\": \"error\",\n                        \"error\": str(e),\n                    })\n\n            return results\n\n        # Start parallel processing in background\n        asyncio.create_task(stream_results())\n\n        # Return acknowledgment immediately\n        return {\n            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n            \"session_id\": session_id,\n            \"intent_count\": len(intent_ids),\n            \"intent_ids\": intent_ids,\n            \"status\": \"dispatched\",\n            \"message\": f\"Dispatched {len(intent_ids)} intents for parallel processing\",\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Dispatch error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Dispatch error: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/escalate\")\nasync def escalate_endpoint(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Escalate an intent to a NEEDLE bead context=context,\n        metadata=metadata,\n    )\n\n    # Store the intent\n    store = await get_store()\n    if utterance_id:\n        intent_id = await store.create_intent(\n            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            project_slug=project_slug,\n            intent_type=intent_type,\n        )\n        escalate_request.intent_id = content={\"error\": f\"Escalate failed: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.get(\"/api/v1/environment\")\nasync def get_environment():\n    \"\"\"Return the discovered environment: all git repos and bead workspaces across all hosts.\"\"\"\n    from .environment.discovery import get_registry\n    import time\n    registry = get_registry()\n    if not registry:\n        return {\"error\": \"Registry not yet initialized\"}\n    entries = [\n        {\n            \"name\": e.name,\n            \"slug\": e.slug,\n            \"path\": str(e.path),\n            \"host\": e.host,\n            \"display_path\": e.display_path,\n            \"has_beads\": e.has_beads,\n            \"remote_url\": e.remote_url,\n            \"remote_name\": e.remote_name,\n        }\n        for e in session_id: str,\n    surface_id: str | None = None,\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Server-Sent Events endpoint for canvas connections.\n\n    Provides real-time updates for:\n    - Topic changes\n    - New results\n    - Intent status updates\n    - Workload summaries\n\n    Sends workload summary on connect for reconnection support.\n    \"\"\"\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n\n    # Get or create session\n    store = await get_store()\n    session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n    if not session:\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        logger.info(f\"Created new session: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Register or update surface\n    if not surface_id:\n        surface_id = await store.register_surface(session_id, \"canvas\")\n        logger.info(f\"Registered canvas surface: {surface_id}\")\n    else:\n        await store.update_surface_heartbeat(surface_id)\n\n    # Create SSE connection\n    connection = broadcaster.register(\n        surface_id=surface_id,\n        session_id=session_id,\n        surface_type=\"canvas\",\n    )\n\n    # Send workload summary on connect\n    summary = await store.get_workload_summary(session_id)\n\n    async def event_stream():\n        try:\n            # Send initial connection event with workload summary\n            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"connected\", {\n                \"surface_id\": surface_id,\n                \"session_id\": session_id,\n            })\n\n            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"workload_summary\", summary)\n\n            # Send initial topic cards\n            if _topic_manager:\n                cards = await _topic_manager.get_active_topic_cards(session_id)\n                yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"topic_cards\", {\n                    \"cards\": [card.to_dict() for card in cards]\n                })\n\n            # Stream events\n            async for event_data in broadcaster.event_generator(connection):\n                yield event_data\n\n        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n            logger.info(f\"SSE stream cancelled for surface {surface_id}\")\n            raise\n\n    return StreamingResponse(\n        event_stream(),\n        media_type=\"text/event-stream\",\n        headers={\n            \"Cache-Control\": \"no-cache\",\n            \"Connection\": \"keep-alive\",\n            \"X-Accel-Buffering\": \"no\",  # Disable nginx buffering\n        },\n    )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/heartbeat\")\nasync def heartbeat(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Heartbeat endpoint for canvas to update surface last_seen.\n\n    Called periodically by the canvas to prevent surface from being marked idle.\n    \"\"\"\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n    surface_id = request.get(\"surface_id\")\n\n    if not session_id or not surface_id:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=400,\n            content={\"error\": \"Missing session_id or surface_id\"}\n        )\n\n    store = await get_store()\n    await store.update_surface_heartbeat(surface_id)\n\n    return {\"status\": \"ok\"}\n\n\n@app.get(\"/topics\")\nasync def get_topics(session_id: str):\n    \"\"\"\n    Get active topic cards for a content={\"error\": \"Topic manager not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    cards = await _topic_manager.get_active_topic_cards(session_id)\n    return {\n        \"topics\": [card.to_dict() for card in cards]\n    }\n\n\n# =============================================================================\n# API v1 Endpoints\n# endpoints\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/surfaces/register\")\nasync def api_v1_register_surface(request: SurfaceRegisterRequest):\n    \"\"\"Register a new surface for a session.\"\"\"\n    store = await get_store()\n\n    # Get or create session\n    session = await store.get_session(request.session_id)\n    if not session:\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        logger.info(f\"Created new session: {session_id}\")\n    else:\n        session_id = request.session_id\n\n    # Register surface\n    surface_id = await store.register_surface(\n        session_id,\n session_id: str,\n    surface_id: Optional[str] = None,\n    surface_type: str = \"canvas\"\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Server-Sent Events endpoint for canvas connections.\n\n    Provides real-time updates for topics, results, and component updates.\n    \"\"\"\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n\n    # Get or create session\n    store = await get_store()\n    session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n    if not session:\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        logger.info(f\"Created new session: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Register or update surface\n    if not surface_id:\n        surface_id = await store.register_surface(session_id, surface_type)\n        logger.info(f\"Registered {surface_type} surface: {surface_id}\")\n    else:\n        await store.update_surface_heartbeat(surface_id)\n\n    # Create SSE connection\n    connection = broadcaster.register(\n        surface_id=surface_id,\n        session_id=session_id,\n        surface_type=surface_type,\n    )\n\n    # Send workload summary on connect\n    summary = await store.get_workload_summary(session_id)\n\n    async def event_stream():\n        try:\n            # Send initial connection event\n            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"connected\", {\n                \"surface_id\": surface_id,\n                \"session_id\": session_id,\n            })\n\n            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"workload_summary\", summary)\n\n            # Send initial topic cards\n            if _topic_manager:\n                cards = await _topic_manager.get_active_topic_cards(session_id)\n                yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"topic_cards\", {\n                    \"cards\": [card.to_dict() for card in cards]\n                })\n\n            # Stream events\n            async for event_data in broadcaster.event_generator(connection):\n                yield event_data\n\n        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n            logger.info(f\"SSE stream cancelled for surface {surface_id}\")\n            raise\n\n    return StreamingResponse(\n        event_stream(),\n        media_type=\"text/event-stream\",\n to parse synthesize response as JSON: {e}\")\n            # Fallback: return minimal result\n            return SynthesizeResult(\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n Extract context for rule evaluation\n171\t        context = {\n172\t            \"environment\": request.metadata.get(\"environment\"),\n173\t            \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n174\t            \"branch\": request.metadata.get(\"branch\"),\n175\t            \"action\": request.metadata.get(\"action\"),\n176\t            \"risk_level\": request.metadata.get(\"risk_level\", \"normal\"),\n177\t        }\n178\t\n179\t        # Check read-only operations (always auto-approved)\n180\t        read_only_actions = auto_approve_rules.get(\"read_only\", [])\n181\t        action = context.get(\"action\")\n182\t        if action in read_only_actions:\n183\t            return True, f\"Read-only operation: {action}\"\n184\t\n185\t        # Check safe mutations with conditions\n186\t        safe_mutations = auto_approve_rules.get(\"safe_mutations\", [])\n187\t        for rule in safe_mutations:\n188\t            condition = rule.get(\"condition\", \"\")\n189\t            actions = rule.get(\"actions\", [])\n190\t\n191\t            # Simple condition evaluation (supports basic comparisons)\n192\t            if self._evaluate_condition(condition, context) and action in actions:\n193\t                return True, f\"Safe mutation in {condition}: {action}\"\n194\t\n195\t        # Check manual approval rules (never auto-approve)\n196\t        manual_rules = exceptions_config.get(\"manual_approval\", [])\n197\t        for rule in manual_rules:\n198\t            condition = rule.get(\"condition\", \"\")\n199\t            actions = rule.get(\"actions\", [])\n200\t\n201\t            # Check if the action is in this rule's actions\n202\t            if action not in actions:\n203\t                continue\n204\t\n205\t            # Evaluate the condition\n206\t            if self._evaluate_condition(condition, context):\n207\t                if rule.get(\"always_approve\") is False:\n208\t                    return False, f\"Manual approval required: {condition}\"\n209\t\n210\t        # Check approval workflow rules\n211\t        approval_config = exceptions_config.get(\"approval\", {})\n212\t        never_auto_approve = approval_config.get(\"never_auto_approve\", [])\n213\t        for condition_str in never_auto_approve:\n214\t            if self._evaluate_condition(condition_str, context):\n215\t                return False, f\"Never auto-approve: {condition_str}\"\n216\t\n217\t        # Default: require manual approval for unknown actions\n218\t        return False, \"Unknown action - requires manual approval\"\n219\t\n220\t    def _evaluate_condition(self, condition: str, context: dict) -> bool:\n221\t        \"\"\"\n222\t        Evaluate a condition string against context.\n223\t\n224\t        Supports simple conditions like:\n225\t        - \"environment == 'staging'\"\n226\t        - \"branch == 'main' || branch == 'master'\"  (using || for OR)\n227\t        - \"action == 'kubectl_delete_namespace'\"\n228\t        - \"environment == 'staging' && action == 'restart'\"  (using && for AND)\n229\t\n230\t        Note: Supports both shell-style operators (||, &&) and Python-style (or, and).\n231\t\n232\t        Args:\n233\t            condition: The condition string\n234\t            context: Dict of available variables\n235\t\n236\t        Returns:\n237\t            True if condition evaluates to True, False otherwise\n238\t        \"\"\"\n239\t        try:\n240\t            # Translate shell-style operators to Python syntax\n241\t            # (People are more familiar with || and && from bash/js)\n242\t            python_condition = condition.replace(\" || \", \" or \").replace(\" && \", \" and \")\n243\t\n244\t            # Create a safe evaluation environment\n245\t            safe_globals = {\"__builtins__\": {}}\n246\t            safe_locals = {\n247\t                \"environment\": context.get(\"environment\"),\n248\t                \"project_slug\": context.get(\"project_slug\"),\n249\t                \"branch\": context.get(\"branch\"),\n250\t                \"action\": context.get(\"action\"),\n251\t                \"risk_level\": context.get(\"risk_level\"),\n252\t            }\n253\t\n254\t            # Evaluate condition\n255\t            result = eval(python_condition, safe_globals, safe_locals)\n256\t            return bool(result)\n257\t        except Exception as e:\n258\t            logger.warning(f\"Failed to evaluate condition '{condition}': {e}\")\n259\t            return False\n260\t\n261\t    def _get_bead_type_from_targets(self, intent_type: str, exceptions_config: dict) -> str:\n262\t        \"\"\"\n263\t        Get the bead type from escalation_targets config.\n264\t\n265\t        Args:\n266\t            intent_type: The intent type from IntentRouter (e.g., 'action', 'task-profile', 'self-modification')\n267\t            exceptions_config: The loaded exceptions.yaml config\n268\t\n269\t        Returns:\n270\t            The bead type to create (default: 'task')\n271\t        \"\"\"\n272\t        # Map IntentRouter types to escalation_targets keys\n273\t        intent_type_mapping = {\n274\t            \"action\": \"action\",\n275\t            \"task-profile\": \"action\",  # Task-profile intents create action-type beads\n276\t            \"self-modification\": \"self_modification\",\n277\t            \"monitoring-config\": \"monitoring_config\",\n278\t        }\n279\t\n280\t        # Map the intent type to escalation_targets key\n281\t        escalation_key = intent_type_mapping.get(intent_type, intent_type)\n282\t\n283\t        escalation_targets = exceptions_config.get(\"escalation_targets\", {})\n284\t        target_config = escalation_targets.get(escalation_key, {})\n285\t        return target_config.get(\"bead_type\", \"task\")\n286\t\n287\t    async def _execute_auto_approved(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n288\t        \"\"\"\n289\t        Execute an auto-approved action directly.\n290\t\n291\t        Executes kubectl and git commands that are auto-approved based on\n292\t        exceptions.yaml rules, without creating beads.\n293\t\n294\t        Args:\n295\t            request: The escalate request\n296\t\n297\t        Returns:\n298\t            Execution result dict\n299\t        \"\"\"\n300\t        action = request.metadata.get(\"action\", \"\")\n301\t        logger.info(f\"Executing auto-approved action '{action}' for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n302\t\n303\t        try:\n304\t            # Route to appropriate executor based on action type\n305\t            if action == \"kubectl_delete_pod\":\n306\t                return await self._execute_delete_pod(request)\n307\t            elif action.startswith(\"kubectl_\"):\n308\t                return await self._execute_kubectl_command(request)\n309\t            elif action.startswith(\"git_\"):\n310\t                return await self._execute_git_command(request)\n311\t            else:\n312\t                # Unknown action - return placeholder for now\n313\t                logger.warning(f\"Unknown auto-approved action: {action}\")\n314\t                return {\n315\t                    \"status\": \"pending\",\n316\t                    \"summary\": f\"Auto-approved action '{action}' not yet implemented\",\n317\t                    \"data\": {\n318\t                        \"action\": action,\n319\t                        \"utterance\": request.utterance,\n320\t                    },\n321\t                    \"urgency\": \"low\",\n322\t                }\n323\t\n324\t        except CommandExecutionError as e:\n325\t            logger.error(f\"Command execution failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n326\t            return {\n327\t                \"status\": \"failed\",\n328\t                \"summary\": f\"Command execution failed: {str(e)}\",\n329\t                \"data\": {\n330\t                    \"action\": action,\n331\t                    \"error\": str(e),\n332\t                },\n333\t                \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n334\t            }\n335\t        except Exception as e:\n336\t            logger.error(f\"Unexpected error executing auto-approved action: {e}\")\n337\t            return {\n338\t                \"status\": \"failed\",\n339\t                \"summary\": f\"Unexpected error: {str(e)}\",\n340\t                \"data\": {\n341\t                    \"action\": action,\n342\t                    \"error\": str(e),\n343\t                },\n344\t                \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n345\t            }\n346\t\n347\t    async def _execute_delete_pod(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n348\t        \"\"\"\n349\t        Execute kubectl delete pod command.\n350\t\n351\t        Args:\n352\t            request: The escalate request\n353\t\n354\t        Returns:\n355\t            Execution result dict\n356\t        \"\"\"\n357\t        executor = get_kubectl_executor()\n358\t\n359\t        # Execute the delete command\n366\t        result = await executor.execute_delete_pod(\n367\t            pod_name=params[\"pod_name\"],\n368\t            namespace=params[\"namespace\"],\n369\t            project_slug=request.project_slug,\n370\t        )\n371\t\n372\t        return result\n373\t\n374\t    async def _execute_kubectl_command(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n375\t        \"\"\"\n376\t        Execute other kubectl commands (placeholder for future).\n377\t\n378\t        Args:\n379\t            request: The escalate request\n380\t\n381\t        Returns:\n382\t            Execution result dict\n383\t        \"\"\"\n384\t        # TODO: Implement other kubectl commands\n385\t        action = request.metadata.get(\"action\", \"\")\n386\t        return {\n387\t            \"status\": \"pending\",\n388\t            \"summary\": f\"Kubectl command '{action}' not yet implemented\",\n389\t            \"data\": {\n390\t                \"action\": action,\n391\t                \"utterance\": request.utterance,\n392\t            },\n393\t            \"urgency\": \"low\",\n394\t        }\n395\t\n396\t    async def _execute_git_command(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n397\t        \"\"\"\n398\t        Execute git commands (placeholder for future).\n399\t\n400\t        Args:\n401\t            request: The escalate request\n402\t\n403\t        Returns:\n404\t            Execution result dict\n405\t        \"\"\"\n406\t        # Build context for the LLM\n431\t        context_parts = [\n432\t            f\"User utterance: {request.utterance}\",\n433\t            f\"Intent type: {request.intent_type}\",\n434\t        ]\n435\t\n436\t        if request.project_slug:\n437\t            context_parts.append(f\"Project: {request.project_slug}\")\n438\t\n439\t        if request.topic_id:\n440\t            context_parts.append(f\"Topic ID: {request.topic_id}\")\n441\t\n442\t        # Add any additional context\n443\t        if request.context:\n444\t            context_parts.append(f\"\\nAdditional context:\")\n445\t            for key, value in request.context.items():\n446\t                context_parts.append(f\"  {key}: {value}\")\n447\t\n448\t        # Build the user message\n449\t        user_message = \"\\n\".join([\n450\t            \"Formulate a NEEDLE bead body for this intent:\",\n451\t            \"\",\n452\t            *context_parts,\n453\t            \"\",\n454\t            \"The bead body should be clear, actionable, and structured for Claude Code execution.\",\n455\t        ])\n456\t\n457\t        logger.info(f\"Formulating bead body for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n458\t\n459\t        # Make LLM call\n460\t        try:\n461\t            bead_body = await client.call_simple(\n462\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n463\t                user_message=user_message,\n464\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n465\t                max_tokens=4096,\n466\t                temperature=0.7,\n467\t            )\n468\t\n469\t            logger.info(f\"Formulated bead body for intent {request.intent_id}: {len(bead_body)} chars\")\n470\t            return bead_body\n471\t\n472\t        except Exception as e:\n473\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\")\n474\t            raise EscalateError(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\") from e\n475\t\n476\t    async def create_bead(\n477\t        self,\n478\t        request: EscalateRequest,\n479\t        bead_body: str,\n480\t    ) -> str:\n481\t        \"\"\"\n482\t        Create a bead using the br CLI.\n483\t\n484\t        Returns the bead ID.\n485\t        \"\"\"\n486\t        # Generate bead title from intent\n487\t        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n488\t\n489\t        # Prepare metadata (includes session_id for bead watcher routing)\n490\t        metadata = {\n491\t            \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n492\t            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n493\t            \"intent_type\": request.intent_type,\n494\t            \"origin_surface_id\": request.metadata.get(\"surface_id\"),\n495\t            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n496\t        }\n497\t\n498\t        if request.project_slug:\n499\t            metadata[\"project_slug\"] = request.project_slug\n500\t\n501\t        if request.topic_id:\n502\t            metadata[\"topic_id\"] = request.topic_id\n503\t\n504\t        # Build br create command\n505\t        # Using br CLI to create the bead\n506\t        # Note: br uses --description for the main body content\n507\t        try:\n508\t            # Build command with description (escape bead body for shell)\n509\t            cmd = [\n510\t                \"br\",\n511\t                \"create\",\n512\t                \"--title\", title,\n513\t                \"--type\", \"task\",  # task-profile intents create task beads\n514\t                \"--description\", bead_body,\n515\t            ]\n516\t\n517\t            # Add labels for metadata tracking\n518\t            # Labels are simple strings, we'll encode key=value for structured data\n519\t            for key, value in metadata.items():\n520\t                if value is not None:\n521\t                    cmd.extend([\"--label\", f\"{key}={value}\"])\n522\t\n523\t            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n524\t                *cmd,\n525\t                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n526\t                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n527\t            )\n528\t\n529\t            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n530\t\n531\t            if proc.returncode != 0:\n532\t                error_msg = stderr.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n533\t                logger.error(f\"br create failed: {error_msg}\")\n534\t                raise BeadCreationError(f\"br create failed: {error_msg}\")\n535\t\n536\t            # Strip whitespace first\n645\t        output = output.strip()\n646\t\n647\t        if not output:\n648\t            logger.warning(f\"Empty br create output\")\n649\t            return str(uuid.uuid4())\n650\t\n651\t        # Try to extract bead ID pattern: lowercase letters, dash, alphanumeric\n652\t        # Bead IDs typically include numbers like: abc-123, xyz-789, adc-2fs\n653\t        # We prioritize matches with digits over letter-only matches\n654\t        patterns = [\n655\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",  # Three components with digit: test-bead-456\n656\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",          # Two components with digit: abc-123\n657\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]+\",                  # )\n747\t\n748\t                # Return result with empty pending_card (auto-approved, no bead created)\n749\t                return EscalateResult(\n750\t                    bead_id=\"\",  # No bead Formulate bead body via LLM (sonnet-class via ZAI proxy)\n2. Create bead using br CLI\n3. Your job is to formulate a clear, actionable NEEDLE bead body from a user's intent.\n\nA NEEDLE bead is a task work item with:\n- A clear title describing what needs to be done\n- A detailed body with context, requirements, and success criteria\n- Proper structure for Claude Code to execute\n\nGiven the user's intent and any available context, produce a bead body that:\n1.
